title: Read low-entropy browser signals
summary: Implement a pure, in-memory reader for basic browser and device signals (language, timezone, screen dimensions, devicePixelRatio) that the scene can consume. The module must never persist or transmit values.

currentState.description: Implementation already exists from US-002 work. src/input/signals.ts exports a BrowserSignals interface and readSignals() function. Tests exist in tests/signals.test.ts.

steps.title: Validate existing test coverage for US-003 acceptance criteria
steps.description: Review and run the existing tests in tests/signals.test.ts to confirm they cover all five acceptance criteria: language, timezone, screen dimensions, devicePixelRatio, and memory-only storage. The US-003 describe block already has 11 tests covering these.
acceptanceCriteriaCovered: All values stay in memory only

steps.title: Verify src/input/signals.ts implementation completeness
steps.description: The existing readSignals() function already reads: navigator.language (fallback 'en'), Intl.DateTimeFormat timezone (fallback 'UTC'), screen.width/height (fallback innerWidth/innerHeight), and devicePixelRatio (nullable). All values are returned as a plain object with no side effects. Confirm no storage APIs are touched and no data is transmitted.
keyDecisions: timezone uses Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone with 'UTC' fallback, wrapped in try-catch
screen dimensions prefer screen.width/height, fall back to innerWidth/innerHeight, then 0
devicePixelRatio uses nullish coalescing to return null when undefined
No localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies, or network calls

steps.title: Run full test suite and build
steps.description: Run all tests to ensure no regressions, then run the production build to confirm TypeScript compiles cleanly and no console errors are introduced.
definitionOfDone: No console errors on normal load
Scene works with and without successful autoplay
Mute toggle and info overlay still work

steps.title: Mark ticket as passing
steps.description: Update prd.json to set US-003 passes=true and update the progress log.

coverage: Happy path: each signal returns expected value from mocked browser APIs
Fallbacks: language empty -> 'en', Intl unavailable -> 'UTC', DPR undefined -> null
Privacy: no localStorage/sessionStorage getItem/setItem calls, no cookie writes
Shape: returned object has exactly the expected keys

risks.risk: jsdom may not perfectly simulate all browser APIs (e.g., screen object)
risks.mitigation: Tests use Object.defineProperty and vi.spyOn to mock specific properties

notes: Implementation and tests already exist from prior work. This ticket primarily requires validation that existing code satisfies all acceptance criteria and marking it as passing.
